Success Criteria: How you know it's done right

Action: Verify the member's identity and account status. Check join date, membership tier, and recent activity log.
Success Criteria: Confirmed member exists in the database, membership is active, no flags on account.
Notes: Pull up their member profile in Circle/Discord/Skool first. If they joined less than 7 days ago, escalate to the onboarding sequence instead.

Action: Review the full conversation history. Read the member's original message or ticket, check any prior interactions, and look up their engagement history.
Success Criteria: Complete understanding of the member's context, issue history, and sentiment before responding.
Notes: Check #feedback channel, DM history, and support ticket system. If member has submitted more than 2 tickets this month, flag for manager review.

Action: Draft a personalized response addressing the member's specific concern. Use their name, reference their history, and answer all questions asked.
Success Criteria: Response addresses every question asked, uses member's name, and sets clear expectations for next steps or resolution time.
Notes: Default templates are OK as a starting point but customize at least the first paragraph. Never paste an unmodified canned response.

Action: Tag the appropriate internal team members if the issue requires escalation beyond community management scope.
Success Criteria: Correct team (Content, Product, Tech, Billing) tagged with relevant context. Priority level clearly communicated in the tag.
Notes: Use @mentions judiciously. Only tag when action from that team is explicitly needed. For urgent issues, tag + send a follow-up DM.

Action: Send the response via the appropriate channel (public reply, DM, or email). Log the interaction and update the member's status.
Success Criteria: Response sent. Interaction logged in the Feedback Collection System or CRM. Member status updated if applicable.
Notes: Public replies build trust (others see you're responsive). Private DMs for sensitive issues like billing disputes or moderation actions.

Action: Set a follow-up reminder based on the issue type and urgency. High-priority items get a 24-hour check-in. Standard items get a 72-hour check-in.
Success Criteria: Reminder created in task management system with all relevant context. Follow-up window matches the priority level.
Notes: Use a recurring check system rather than relying on memory. A missed follow-up erodes trust faster than a slow first response.

Action: Execute the follow-up check-in. Confirm resolution or escalate if the issue is still open. Close the loop with a brief summary.
Success Criteria: Member confirms resolution OR escalation path is activated. Ticket status updated to Resolved or Escalated.
Notes: Always ask: 'Did this resolve your concern?' A closed ticket doesn't mean a resolved concern. If member doesn't respond within 48 hours, send one more check-in then close.

Action: Document any patterns or insights from this interaction in the weekly ops review doc. Note any process improvements suggested by this case.
Success Criteria: One-paragraph summary added to the weekly review doc. Any process improvement ideas are captured as actionable suggestions.
Notes: This step prevents recurring issues from being treated as one-offs. Three similar tickets in a month = a system problem, not a people problem.

Action: Review the weekly ops doc and identify recurring themes or systemic issues across all interactions from the past 7 days.
Success Criteria: At least 3 recurring patterns identified or confirmed none exist. Action items created for systemic fixes.
Notes: Look for patterns across tickets: multiple members asking the same question means your documentation isn't clear. Multiple billing complaints means your checkout flow needs attention.

Action: Archive the resolved ticket and update any relevant knowledge base articles, FAQs, or help docs based on what was learned.
Success Criteria: Knowledge base updated reflecting the resolution. FAQ or help doc revised if this interaction revealed gaps. Ticket archived with complete resolution notes.
Notes: This step closes the loop permanently. If you don't update your knowledge base, the same question comes back next week from a different member.
